channel.title: La Cyber Sphère — Bulletin de veille cyber
channel.description: Alertes critiques et élevées, résumées et sourcées.

item.title: [Élevé] Pro-Russia hacktivist activity continues to target UK organisations
item.description: The NCSC encourages local government and critical infrastructure operators to harden their ‘denial of service’ (DoS) defences

item.title: [Élevé] NCSC issues warning over hacktivist groups disrupting UK organisations and online services
item.description: Russian‑aligned hacktivist groups continue to target UK organisations with disruptive cyber attacks

item.title: [Élevé] Exploitation of Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN
item.description: Agencies strongly encourage immediate investigation of potential compromise of Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN.

item.title: [Faible] Alert: NCSC advises UK organisations to take action following conflict in the Middle East
item.description: In response to the evolving events in the Middle East, the NCSC is advising that UK organisations review their cyber security posture.

item.title: [Faible] International security chiefs to convene in Glasgow for flagship CYBERUK conference
item.description: CYBERUK will be delivered by the NCSC and sponsors across four distinct tracks of activity: Resilience, Technology, Threat, and Ecosystem.

item.title: [Faible] NCSC CEO: Seize 'disruptive' vibe coding opportunity to make software more secure
item.description: Dr Richard Horne delivered a keynote about cyber risks and opportunities at the RSAC Conference in San Francisco

item.title: [Faible] Vulnerabilities affecting Citrix NetScaler ADC and Citrix NetScaler Gateway
item.description: UK organisations encouraged to take immediate action to mitigate two recently disclosed vulnerabilities affecting Citrix NetScaler ADC and Citrix NetScaler Gateway.

item.title: [Critique] Vulnerability affecting F5 BIG-IP APM
item.description: The NCSC is encouraging UK organisations to mitigate an unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability affecting F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager.

item.title: [Faible] NCSC warns of messaging app targeting
item.description: The NCSC has issued actions for individuals at risk of targeted attacks against messaging apps.

item.title: [Faible] APT28 exploit routers to enable DNS hijacking operations
item.description: Russian cyber actor APT28 exploit vulnerable routers to hijack DNS, enabling adversary‑in‑the‑middle attacks and theft of passwords and authentication tokens.

item.title: [Faible] UK exposes Russian military intelligence hijacking vulnerable routers for cyber attacks
item.description: New advisory warns cyber threat group APT28 have exploited vulnerable edge devices to support malicious operations.

item.title: [Faible] Cyber chief: UK faces "perfect storm" for cyber security
item.description: As the technology landscape develops, the definition of cyber security is expanding with it.

item.title: [Faible] World-first NCSC-engineered device secures vulnerable display links
item.description: SilentGlass, a plug-and-play device, actively blocks any unexpected or malicious HDMI and Display Port connections.

item.title: [Faible] International cyber agencies share fresh advice to defend against China-linked covert networks
item.description: New advisory highlights how to defend against attacker tactics believed to be used by China-linked actors to hide malicious cyber activity.

item.title: [Faible] NCSC: Leave passwords in the past - passkeys are the future
item.description: Passkeys are the more secure and user-friendly login method and should be the default authentication option for consumers.

item.title: [Élevé] Defending against China-nexus covert networks of compromised devices
item.description: Explaining the widespread shift in tactics, techniques and procedures (TTPs) towards networks of compromised infrastructure, and how to defend against it

item.title: [Élevé] Executive Summary: Defending against China-nexus covert networks of compromised devices
item.description: Organisations should map and baseline their edge device traffic, especially VPN and remote access connections, and adopt dynamic threat feed filtering that includes known covert network indicators.

item.title: [Faible] NCSC CEO: Hostile states linked to three-quarters of cyber attacks affecting UK's critical systems
item.description: Dr Richard Horne highlighted the scale of cyber threats against the UK’s critical infrastructure at RUSI’s Annual Security Lecture.

item.title: [Faible] Alert: NCSC issues advice following global targeting of Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways
item.description: Organisations using Fortinet services are being urged to take action following a campaign affecting firewalls and VPN gateways.

item.title: [Faible] The AI shift in cyber risk: why leaders must act now
[Faible] Cash App owner to pay $45 million to settle allegations of lax security
item.description: State attorneys general announced the bipartisan agreement with Block, Inc. on Wednesday, saying that the company incorrectly promised users that Cash App offered the same protections as a bank.

item.title: [Faible] Greek victims file lawsuit against Intellexa over Predator spyware
item.description: The use of the spyware came to light in 2022, with traces of Predator found on dozens of phones. The scandal led to the resignation of Greece’s intelligence service chief and the prime minister’s chief of staff.

item.title: [Faible] EU takes member states to court over unimplemented cybersecurity law
item.description: Ireland, Spain, France and the Netherlands are more than 20 months late in transposing the NIS2 Directive for the cybersecurity of critical infrastructure.

item.title: [Faible] NSA revives 'Tailored Access Operations' name for elite hacking unit
item.description: NSA last week changed the moniker of its Office of Computer Network Operations (CNO) back to Tailored Access Operations (TAO), a name that is sure to elicit nostalgia among the broader digital community for a group with…

item.title: [Critique] Latvian forestry company still restoring systems weeks after ransomware attack
item.description: A foreign, financially motivated group was responsible for a cyberattack on state-owned forestry company Latvijas Valsts Mezi (LVM), officials said.

item.title: [Élevé] École Supérieure de Génie Informatique : 26 451 dossiers étudiants en fuite
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à l’ESGI, l’École Supérieure de Génie Informatique, a été revendiquée sur un forum... L’article École Supérieure de Génie Informatique : 26 451 dossiers étudiants en fuite est apparu en…

item.title: [Faible] Pays du Mont-Blanc : une cyberattaque chez son opérateur paralyse les services
item.description: La Communauté de Communes Pays du Mont-Blanc (CCPMB) a confirmé être touchée par les conséquences d’une cyberattaque de... L’article Pays du Mont-Blanc : une cyberattaque chez son opérateur paralyse les services est…

item.title: [Élevé] Atout France : plus de 400 000 comptes et plusieurs accès administrateurs revendiqués après une faille IDOR
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à Atout France, l’agence chargée du développement touristique de la France, a été... L’article Atout France : plus de 400 000 comptes et plusieurs accès administrateurs revendiqués après…

item.title: [Faible] Spiko : pièces d’identité et selfies de clients exposés après une cyberattaque chez Onfido
item.description: Spiko informe certains de ses clients d’une potentielle violation de données personnelles liée à Onfido, désormais intégré à... L’article Spiko : pièces d’identité et selfies de clients exposés après une cyberattaque…

item.title: [Élevé] Vaccination scolaire en Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes : données d’enfants et numéros de Sécurité sociale exposés
item.description: Une fuite de données touche la plateforme utilisée en Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes pour recueillir les autorisations parentales dans le cadre... L’article Vaccination scolaire en Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes : données d’enfants et…

item.title: [Critique] SNOSM : 11 682 fichiers et code source en fuite après une cyberattaque
item.description: Une revendication particulièrement lourde vise le Système national d’observation de la sécurité en montagne (SNOSM). Publiée le 9... L’article SNOSM : 11 682 fichiers et code source en fuite après une cyberattaque est…

item.title: [Élevé] SDAG Adhésifs : 58 725 enregistrements de commandes et 3 653 clients dans une fuite de données
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à Boutique SDAG a été revendiquée sur un forum cybercriminel le 8 juillet... L’article SDAG Adhésifs : 58 725 enregistrements de commandes et 3 653 clients dans une fuite de données est…

item.title: [Élevé] Fédération Française Handisport : près de 60 000 licenciés exposés après une cyberattaque
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à la Fédération Française Handisport a été revendiquée sur un forum cybercriminel le... L’article Fédération Française Handisport : près de 60 000 licenciés exposés après une cyberattaque…

item.title: [Élevé] Moove : passeports et pièces d’identité de passagers en fuite après une cyberattaque
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à FlyMoove, plateforme française spécialisée dans l’aviation d’affaires et la mobilité aérienne régionale,... L’article Moove : passeports et pièces d’identité de passagers en fuite après…

item.title: [Élevé] Association.fr : 160 000 dirigeants associatifs et des IBAN dans une fuite de données
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à Association.fr, site d’information consacré au secteur associatif français et édité par HelloAsso,... L’article Association.fr : 160 000 dirigeants associatifs et des IBAN dans une fuite…

item.title: [Faible] Hackers Use Fake Microsoft Entra Passkey Enrollment to Gain Microsoft 365 Access
item.description: A threat actor has been targeting organizations spanning multiple sectors with voice-based fake security requests that prompt Microsoft 365 users to enroll a new Entra passkey with an aim to carry out data extortion…

item.title: [Faible] Study of 281 Free Android VPN Apps Finds Traffic Leaks, Unencrypted Data, and Tracking
item.description: Researchers ran 281 of the most popular free VPN apps on the Google Play Store through a new testing system and found that many fail at the basics people install a VPN for, i.e., keeping their traffic private and…

item.title: [Faible] Exposed Hacker Server Reveals WP-SHELLSTORM Backdooring Thousands of WordPress Sites
item.description: A cybercrime crew left one of its own servers wide open on the internet for three weeks, and it exposed the operation's inner workings: the hacking tools, the activity logs, and target lists naming more than 1.4 million…

item.title: [Faible] From 17,000 to 1.1 Million Assets: How Lumen Technologies Rebuilt Exposure Management at Scale
item.description: Most enterprises assume their asset inventory is close enough to accurate. The evidence suggests otherwise. According to a survey of over 600 security leaders in the 2026 Axonius Actionability Report, only 45% of…

item.title: [Moyen] Unpatched XRING Flaw in XQUIC Lets Remote Clients Crash HTTP/3 Servers
item.description:
A single wrong variable on one line in XQUIC, Alibaba's QUIC and HTTP/3 library, lets any remote client crash the server with a short burst of completely legal traffic. There is no patch.
FoxIO researcher Sébastien…

item.title: [Critique] Zimbra urges customers to patch critical web client XSS flaw
item.description: The Zimbra security team urged customers to patch a critical vulnerability affecting the Classic Web Client used to access the Zimbra Collaboration suite. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Former ransomware negotiator gets 4 years for BlackCat attacks
item.description: A former employee of cybersecurity incident response company DigitalMint was sentenced to 70 months in prison for targeting U.S. companies in BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware attacks. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Ransomware Negotiator Gets 70 Months in Prison for Aiding BlackCat Attacks
item.description: A 41-year-old former ransomware negotiator has been sentenced to nearly six years (i.e., 70 months) in prison in the U.S. for their role in conspiring with the now-defunct BlackCat ransomware operators to extort…

item.title: [Moyen] Attackers Exploit 'Ill Bloom' Vulnerability to Drain $3.1 Million From Cryptocurrency Wallets
item.description: Security firm Coinspect has disclosed a crypto wallet flaw it calls Ill Bloom, and attackers are already using it. The flaw is in how some wallet software generated its recovery phrase, the words that…

item.title: [Élevé] Injective SDK on npm infected with cryptocurrency wallet stealer
item.description: Hackers compromised the Injective Labs SDK project's GitHub repository and used it to publish a malicious package on the Node Package Manager (npm) that stole cryptocurrency wallet private keys and mnemonic seed…

item.title: [Faible] OpenMandriva Linux says contributor tried to sabotage the project
item.description: The OpenMandriva Linux project announced that it was the target of an attempted act of internal sabotage after a dispute among contributors. [...]

item.title: [Critique] New GigaWiper Windows Backdoor Bundles Disk Wiping, Fake Ransomware, and Spyware
item.description: Microsoft has taken apart a destructive Windows backdoor it calls GigaWiper. What stands out is how it is built: not one tool but three older destructive programs bolted into one, offered as commands the operator can…

item.title: [Élevé] Dormant GitHub Accounts Help Attackers Blend In While Mapping Corporate Orgs
item.description:
Datadog Security Labs is warning of "several overlapping campaigns" that are systematically enumerating corporate GitHub organizations, repositories, and user accounts through the GitHub API.
"Operators rely on…

item.title: [Faible] FFE : une nouvelle fuite massive à la veille des Championnats de France
item.description: Une revendication attribuée à un pirate vise la Fédération française d’équitation, avec des données exposées et un message politique assumé en plein Championnats de France d’équitation.

item.title: [Faible] Mount Royal University confirms breach as hackers claim attack
item.description: Mount Royal University in Calgary says hackers stole and then deleted data from its file storage systems after breaching the university's network. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Microsoft patches RoguePlanet Defender zero-day vulnerability
item.description: Microsoft has released a security patch to address a Defender zero-day vulnerability known as "RoguePlanet," disclosed after the June 2026 Patch Tuesday. [...]

item.title: [Élevé] AssuranceAmerica data breach exposes records of 6.9 million drivers
item.description: American insurance company AssuranceAmerica has disclosed a data breach impacting nearly 7 million drivers after attackers gained access to its systems earlier this year. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Police arrests 5,800 suspects in global anti-fraud crackdown
item.description: Law enforcement agencies have arrested 5,811 suspects and seized $293 million in illicit assets in a global anti-fraud operation spanning 97 countries. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Microsoft to retire the OWA Light client in Exchange Server
item.description: Microsoft has announced plans to disable Outlook Web Access (OWA) Light, the lightweight version of the Outlook Web App email client, in a future Exchange Server update. [...]

item.title: [Faible] The Hidden Security Risks of Reduced Summer IT Coverage
item.description: Security operations don't slow down when IT teams take vacation, but staffing levels often do. Kaseya explains how AI-driven automation can help organizations maintain consistent security operations and reduce reliance…

item.title: [Faible] New Forg365 phishing platform uses AI to target Microsoft 365 accounts
item.description: A new phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) operation called Forg365 focuses on stealing Microsoft 365 accounts by combining adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) and device code methods with AI-assisted lure generation. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Microsoft expects more Windows security updates from AI-discovered flaws
item.description: Microsoft says Windows users should expect to see an increase in security updates as the company increasingly relies on artificial intelligence to discover vulnerabilities in its codebase. [...]

item.title: [Faible] New Helix vishing group emerges in SharePoint data theft attacks
item.description: A new data-extortion group called Helix is using identity-focused tactics such as voice phishing (vishing), device code phishing, and multi-factor authentication (MFA) abuse to steal data from SharePoint environments.…

item.title: [Faible] Fake 7-Zip Installers Turn Devices Into Residential Proxy Nodes
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new threat actor dubbed Lurking Lizard that has been operating an end-to-end malicious residential proxy business using an infrastructure comprising more than 230…

item.title: [Faible] GhostApproval Symlink Flaws Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code in AI Coding Agents
item.description: Researchers at Wiz found that a flaw in six popular AI coding assistants lets a booby-trapped code project quietly take control of a developer's computer. The assistant asks permission to edit one…

item.title: [Critique] Top AI Agents Built to Catch Malicious Code Can Be Tricked Into Running It
item.description:
Ask an AI coding agent to scan open-source code for security holes, and it might run the attacker's code on your own machine instead.
That is the finding in a proof-of-concept published Wednesday by the AI…

item.title: [Faible] Meta's New AI Image Tool Lets Others Use Your Public Instagram Photos in AI Images
item.description:
Meta has announced that its new artificial intelligence (AI) model Muse Image lets people use public Instagram posts and reels to generate AI content, and it's enabled by default.
"You can also @-mention Instagram…

item.title: [Moyen] Microsoft Patches RoguePlanet Defender Flaw That Can Grant SYSTEM Privileges
item.description:
Microsoft has released security updates for a Defender vulnerability known as RoguePlanet, nearly a month after details of the flaw became public.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-50656 (CVSS score: 7.8), is a…

item.title: [Critique] GodDamn Ransomware Uses PoisonX Driver to Disable Endpoint Defenses
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new ransomware family called GodDamn that employs the PoisonX kernel driver to neutralize security software as part of its defense evasion strategy.
According to a new report…

item.title: [Faible] Summer of Clearinghouses
item.description: Everyone seems to have announced a clearinghouse over the past few weeks. We did too. Ours is called Athena, and the main thing that sets it apart is that it was already real and running when we announced it — built…

item.title: [Faible] AI Attacks Move in Minutes. Join This Webinar on Building a Defense That Keeps Up
item.description: AI has changed how fast attacks move. Work that once took an attacker days now takes minutes. Using models like Mythos, attackers write tailored bait, pick targets, test what lands, and jump to the next host before your…

item.title: [Faible] ThreatsDay: Cloud Bucket Hijacking, Windows LPE Chain, Global Fraud Bust + 17 More Stories
item.description:
Most security mess starts as admin work. A link gets clicked. A tool gets trusted. A bucket name gets reused. A setting stays loose because nobody wants to touch it.
This week is full of that kind of damage. Not…

item.title: [Faible] npm 12 Disables Install Scripts by Default to Reduce Supply Chain Risk
item.description:
GitHub has officially announced the release of npm version 12 with install scripts disabled by default, along with deprecating granular access tokens (GATs) designed to bypass two-factor authentication (2FA).
The…
